focus: Slice frustration and success stories from forums

PAIN.quote: Nothing I do fixes my slice..please help
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice thread title

PAIN.quote: I still send 70%+ of my drives with either a fade or it starts right and slices hard right
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I replaced the lighter Alta shaft with the Hazardous Black stiff shaft. I even bought a heavier weight for the club head thinking a combination of stiff shaft + heavy weight head + lower loft would get me to hit less slices. Nothing has worked
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice
PAIN.context: Equipment changes failed to fix slice

PAIN.quote: I go to the range and tinker all day long and nothing works consistently
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice
PAIN.context: Range time not translating to results

PAIN.quote: I took a lesson, he corrected my grip and turned my left hand over the grip, my thumbs are no longer straight down the grip. He even told me to close my front shoulder. Still, slice/push everything
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I already got with a instructor, he changed my grip and closed my front shoulder. Still nothing consistent even after multiple range trips and rounds. That's why I'm confused. Felt like I was fed a bunch of nonsense
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice
PAIN.context: Frustration with instruction - felt like nonsense

PAIN.quote: For everyone saying the G400 Max is a 'fairway finder'....not for me. I find nothing easier/better about this driver
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: I just can't square the clubface no matter if I have a stiff shaft/heavier club head and a rolled over grip
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice

PAIN.quote: Here are my averages for 14 drives. Nothing I do seems to fix this slice I'm dealing with
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice
PAIN.context: Launch monitor data confirming persistent slice

PAIN.quote: My buddy Sterling hits a big ol nasty slice. I said 'Even take a lesson?' and everyone laughed like it was a joke. It wasn't a joke. He still hasn't taken a lesson and still hits a slice
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

PAIN.quote: I don't know anyone that has fixed a slice on their own; I tried... didn't work
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

PAIN.quote: Trying to fix more than one issue at a time is a recipe for bad golf and frustration
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

PAIN.quote: I had extreme case of bad slice with driver. It was so bad that if I swing as hard as I can without worrying about anything, I could probably send the ball 2 fairways to the right
I was gripping like a weak grandma and open face-biased grip
I started golf earlier this year and had a bad slice and a 25 handicap at the end of the season
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

HOPE.quote: After I hit my first draw of my life, I had to go back to my car to change my underwear
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

HOPE.quote: if my shots were 9/10 slice, now its 8/10 straight draw, with 2/10 slice shots. This is a major improvement
BOOM. Nearly dead-straight draw shots everywhere
Those ones... well... oh dear sweet lord. I would say good 40 yard gain. This tells me I still have to master my driver
Good luck with all of you slice-happy golfers. I know how frustrating it is, but really. Try everything. I mean ALL of them. If that doesn't work, that's when you need professional help
I massively struggled with the driver for the first half of last year. I had to literally break my swing/stance/grip/alignment down to basics to fix it. Now I fixed it, but it was frustrating
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice
HOPE.context: Full reset required but ultimately successful

HOPE.quote: After I got used to the different grip, whenever I hit it with slight draw, straight ball flight = Good 10 yard gain from my usual driver distance
Im just amazed of what the grip can do. Play around with it. I know you'll get something out of it

Lately my drives have been averaging 230-250 feels like I'm swiping the ball, good shots light fades... after implementing the 2 things above I'm hitting straight to slight draw shots, distance is more in the 270-280 range, hit one yesterday on a downhill par 5 360
when I focus on rotating my hands through impact, I have a straight, sometimes draw, ball flight
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: You are steep and over the top. At best you will pull the ball if you can square the face
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Diagnosis: steep and over the top

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Your issue is either in your setup, backswing, or transition to downswing... or maybe a problem in all three
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: A slice is caused by an open face at impact. A sound grip will allow you to square the clubface without any conscious effort
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Open face is root cause - grip is solution

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: all I was missing was rotating of the wrists right before impact
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: My biggest problem with slicing has been keeping my grip too firm on the club, which resulting in my wrists being stiff to flip over on the downswing
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Almost 1200rpm side spin... isn't really ok. Face or path issue or both
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Get a DRAW biased / HT driver. HUGE difference. HUGGGE difference. Slice was still there. Remember, this is JUST AN AID, not the solution
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Draw driver helps but doesn't solve

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: CHANGE YOUR GRIP & GRIP IT STRONG. This officially, solved my slicing issue
I rotated the grip clockwise by 2 thumbs. I gripped it strong, and swung

The common problem with a slice is that a golfer leaves their clubface open at impact. To make it simpler, wear a watch on your bottom hand, with the face on the underside. If you can see the face of the watch through impact then you won't slice
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I just tell myself to focus on that, And its great cause everything else that might be in your head before driving is cleared
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: My pro now has me trying to feel like I'm turning the knuckles of the lead hand down through impact to promote the closing of the clubhead
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I took it one step at a time, working on one thing and not all at once. Most of what I fixed was my grip (lead hand rotated to see three knuckles) and shallowing my swing
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: The thing my PGA pro showed me was holding a club parallel to the ground and turning the grip like a screwdriver to screw something into the wall
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I upgraded from my old Callaway ERC fusion 9degree driver to a TM R7 Draw 10.5degree driver. HUGE difference
R7 draw irons' distance isn't that good. Kinda like R7 Draw driver

VILLAIN.quote: I wouldn't necessarily call the draw driver a swing aid...more like a band aide and those fall off
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

VILLAIN.quote: Be careful with too much hand or wrist action. This rotation is very subtle. Get too carried away with it and you will be a hook machine
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good

VILLAIN.quote: Rolling the wrists is timing dependent and the ball sprays anywhere if your timing is not perfect
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good
VILLAIN.context: Timing issues with manual wrist roll

sources_covered: GolfWRX - Nothing I do fixes my slice
TheSandTrap - Finally Fixed my slice for good
MyGolfSpy Forum - Please help fix my slice
high_value_themes_identified: Lesson advice often doesn't translate to results
Equipment changes alone don't fix slice
Strong grip change is most cited fix
First draw moment is life-changing emotional event
40+ yard distance gains possible after fix
Draw drivers are band-aids not solutions
